pull.title: Implement BIP 370 PSBTv2
pull.body: BIP 370 PSBTv2 introduces several new fields and different invariants for PSBT. This PR implements those new fields and restructures the PSBT implementation to match PSBTv2 but still remain compatible with PSBTv0.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Code Coverage & Benchmarks For details see: https://corecheck.dev/bitcoin/bitcoin/pulls/21283. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | Concept ACK | [rkrux](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283#issuecomment-2639461217), [theStack](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283#issuecomment-4091861319) | | Stale ACK | [scgbckbone](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283#issuecomment-1757763991) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore. ### Conflicts Reviewers, this pull request conflicts with the following ones: * [#34917](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34917) (wallet: mark bip125-replaceable deprecated, remove walletrbf argument by rkrux) * [#34893](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34893) (psbt: preserve proprietary fields when combining PSBTs by w0xlt) * [#34773](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34773) (test: migrate functional test equality asserts to `assert_equal` by l0rinc) * [#34520](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34520) (refactor: Add [[nodiscard]] to functions returning bool+mutable ref by maflcko) * [#34124](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34124) (validation: make `CCoinsView` a pure virtual interface by l0rinc) * [#33014](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33014) (rpc: Fix internal bug in descriptorprocesspsbt when encountering invalid signatures by b-l-u-e) If you consider this pull request important, please also help to review the conflicting pull requests. Ideally, start with the one that should be merged first. ### LLM Linter (✨ experimental) Possible places where named args for integral literals may be used (e.g. `func(x, /*named_arg=*/0)` in C++, and `func(x, named_arg=0)` in Python): - `PSBTInput psbt_input(0, Txid{}, 0);` in `src/test/fuzz/deserialize.cpp` - `assert_raises_rpc_error(-4, "...", wallet.walletcreatefundedpsbt, [{'txid': utxo1['previous_txid'], 'vout': utxo1['previous_vout']}], {target_address: 1}, 0, {'add_inputs': False})` in `test/functional/rpc_psbt.py` - `assert_raises_rpc_error(-4, "Insufficient funds", wallet.walletcreatefundedpsbt, [{'txid': utxo1['previous_txid'], 'vout': utxo1['previous_vout']}], {target_address: 1}, 0, {'add_inputs': True, 'minconf': 3, 'fee_rate': 10})` in `test/functional/rpc_psbt.py` Possible places where comparison-specific test macros should replace generic comparisons: - [test/functional/rpc_psbt.py] `+ assert len(psbt1_decoded['inputs'][0].keys()) > 3` -> use `assert_greater_than(len(psbt1_decoded['inputs'][0].keys()), 3)` - [test/functional/rpc_psbt.py] `+ assert len(psbt1_decoded['inputs'][1].keys()) == 3` -> use `assert_equal(len(psbt1_decoded['inputs'][1].keys()), 3)` - [test/functional/rpc_psbt.py] `+ assert len(psbt2_decoded['inputs'][0].keys()) == 3` -> use `assert_equal(len(psbt2_decoded['inputs'][0].keys()), 3)` - [test/functional/rpc_psbt.py] `+ assert len(psbt2_decoded['inputs'][1].keys()) > 3` -> use `assert_greater_than(len(psbt2_decoded['inputs'][1].keys()), 3)` 2026-03-28 00:09:37

In c8b879609f02288975e3a6f714fbcfa1a93edcbc (*moveonly: Move (Un)Serialize(To/From)Vector, (De)SerializeHDKeypaths to psbt module*), should you possibly move/copy `#include <streams.h>`, `#include <span.h>` and `#include <script/keyorigin.h>`?

In commit 0bd656b64e72e29f255a860c71c542f5a9f18c17 (*Separate individual HD Keypath serialization into separate functions*)

Should line:

```cpp
if (value_len % 4 || value_len == 0) {
```

be

```cpp
if (value_len % 4 != 0 || value_len == 0) {
```

to make it more explicit?

@kiminuo



> In commit [0bd656b](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/commit/0bd656b64e72e29f255a860c71c542f5a9f18c17) (_Separate individual HD Keypath serialization into separate functions_)
> 
> Should line:
> 
> ```c++
> if (value_len % 4 || value_len == 0) {
> ```
> 
> be
> 
> ```c++
> if (value_len % 4 != 0 || value_len == 0) {
> ```
> 
> to make it more explicit?

This is moved code, so I will not be changing it.



> Is it possible that in commit [459fd3b](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/commit/459fd3b0cc646f5ab02dd3cfd239c7a0418e6a32) (*Implement operator< for KeyOriginInfo and CExtPubKey *)
> 
> ```c++
> else if (a.pubkey > b.pubkey) {
>     return false;
> }
> ```
> 
> is missing?

Done.

***

In the future, please leave such comments on the code as inline comments rather than comments like this. It makes it much easier to respond directly to specific questions such as these.

Looking good so far. 

Ran this against CLN test suite and got no unexpected behavior.
Needs rebase, if still relevant.
ACK https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283/commits/93086378db3d0e959cf2b73e9a7a9880022043c1

```
psbt.cpp: In member function ‘CMutableTransaction PartiallySignedTransaction::GetUnsignedTx() const’:
psbt.cpp:107:35: error: ‘transaction_identifier<has_witness>::transaction_identifier(const uint256&) [with bool has_witness = false]’ is private within this context
  107 |         txin.prevout.hash = input.prev_txid;
      |                                   ^~~~~~~~~
Will split this up

Caveat: I'm not a C++ dev and don't know much about Core development.

I did however check the changes here against my Rust implementation of the same, and found some bugs in mine, so cheers!
Aside from the existential question, it would be useful to have some projects to test this PR against: https://bitcoin.stackexchange.com/questions/125384/who-uses-or-wants-to-use-psbtv2-bip370
cc @dangould

@Sjors: You want to know who wants this? I've been wanting to use Bitcoin Core to decode and manipulate the PSBTs that Core Lightning stores in its database, but it only works for the older, first-gen PSBTs. Core Lightning moved on to PSBTv2 some time ago, so now all the newer PSBTs fail to parse in Bitcoin Core. I've been having to hack together various half-baked tools I've found around the Internet, but it would be so good if Bitcoin Core would be upgraded to the current spec.
@whitslack yes I authored the migration, and v2<->v0 roundtripping is supported in libwally as well. Would be nice to move the industry a bit closer to making v2 the defacto default.
@whitslack @instagibbs thanks for mentioning CLN. Is it possible to turn off its round tripping so I can test this PR against it? And are there any good nodes on signet or testnet4?

@bigspider thanks for your [reply](https://groups.google.com/g/bitcoindev/c/OPx9qvmr5Nc/m/8mp8VHjQBQAJ) on the mailinglist. So I guess another way to test this PR is to patch HWI to not do the conversion between PSBT 0 and 2, and then make (any?) transaction with a Ledger that runs at least 2.0.0.

> @bigspider thanks for your [reply](https://groups.google.com/g/bitcoindev/c/OPx9qvmr5Nc/m/8mp8VHjQBQAJ) on the mailinglist. So I guess another way to test this PR is to patch HWI to not do the conversion between PSBT 0 and 2, and then make (any?) transaction with a Ledger that runs at least 2.0.0.

I suppose so, although it feels somewhat circular as bitcoin-core was always the reference for the tests of the Ledger Bitcoin app!

Coldcard Mk4/Q also support PSBT v2 if you need to test ( from October 2023 https://github.com/Coldcard/firmware/blob/master/releases/History-Mk4.md#520---2023-10-10)

but as @bigspider, I also used core PR to implement this

IIUC the recently merged Silent Payments PSBT BIP _only_ works with PSBTv2?
https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0375.mediawiki

Having a (rough) draft PR implementing those fields on top of this PR could also aid in review. cc @josibake @andrewtoth

> IIUC the recently merged Silent Payments PSBT BIP _only_ works with PSBTv2?

Suggested to consider renaming BIP375 to be more clear about this, unless there's a reason I'm missing: https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/1687#discussion_r1913779155

I briefly tested a Ledger NanoS with Bitcoin Testnet app 2.2.5 by disabling its v0 <-> v2 conversion: https://github.com/Sjors/HWI/commit/28bfb53e136918e5c6279b6bc6ceb33e1421b934

On master this triggers a "failed to sign" error. With this PR (rebased) it works.

> it feels somewhat circular

Agreed, an independent implementation would be nice to test against.

(I also read BIP 370 again, so should be in a better position now to review this PR)

> Is it possible to turn off its round tripping so I can test this PR against it?

Internally it *only* uses v2, but it will output v0 if you ask for it.

The `fundchannel_start` -> `fundchannel_complete id psbt` workflow is the easiest to test. If I give it a v2 PSBT, it's not going to be smart and try to fix encoding mistakes?

Tested that flow, and also the `sendpsbt` RPC.

So this PR makes the GUI produce v2 PSBTs by default. We might want to change that, or at least have a downgrade checkbox. Similarly the `send` RPC and friends should have a way to force a lower version.

---

Here's a branch that:
* makes v0 the GUI default: it's too much work to add a checkbox everywhere. Later on for Silent Payments we can just make v2 the default if an sp address is involved.
* adds a `psbt_version` option to `send`, `sendall`, `converttopsbt`, `bumpfee` and `psbtbumpfee`

https://github.com/Sjors/bitcoin/tree/2025/01/psbtv2

> If I give it a v2 PSBT, it's not going to be smart and try to fix encoding mistakes?

That would be a libwally question. I don't recall.
Some feedback on the first three commits.

Definite Concept ACK

This one is a biggie, I should get around to reviewing this PR sometime later this month and will leave a review then.

Edit: Could not get to it before, it will take me some time for me to start reviewing it.

Studied the other commits as well.

Perhaps there's an opportunity to split this PR by first introducing the internal refactoring that anticipates PSBTv2. But other that I don't think it can be made much smaller.

In 20 days it will have been 5 years since this PR was open. Let's try to re-gain some momentum around reviewing this?

If a few other regular contributors volunteer to review this, i will commit to review it too. Some (re?)testing from users and outside contributors could be useful too.

I've ran a mutation testing analysis for this PR - mostly for `src/psbt.cpp`. The results were not great, less than 50% of mutation score. Left some comments if some unkilled mutants (changes that are not detected by any test). Let me know if they're useful or not useful at all.

Ping @rkrux @bigspider @nervana21 @erickcestari @macgyver13 @codeabysss since you reacted to my [comment](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283#issuecomment-3843800388) above. If you want to help get this through the finish line, momentum is now. (And not all review need to inspect the code in details, for instance reporting successful compatibility testing with other projects is useful as well!)
Oh that's a neat idea. I'll see if some compatibility testing can surface any issues.

Rebased for #34725 

Will be addressing comments soon(tm), likely after 31.0 branching for major things.

For the unkilled mutants, all of those would benefit from having tests for `AddInput` and `AddOutput`, both of which are pretty much untested for v2 as there are no unit tests for them and the RPCs currently restrict to v0. I'll add unit tests for those eventually.

Some more comments as i finished going through the commits one by one. I also further improved the fuzz harness, will edit the patch in my previous comment. Been running it for a few days now on a beefy machine with no issue.

I've ended up significantly changing the order of commits and made the invariants around required field availability stronger. Specifically, I've made it so that input prev_txid and prev_out, and output amount and script, are all required and enforced by constructors. This has resulted in removing the default constructors and reworking everything to use either the constructors that take those fields or the deserializing constructor.

I've additionally dropped the global unsigned transaction field and it will instead be constructed for PSBTv0 from the component parts. This makes it so that all PSBTs are internally represented in the PSBTv2 form and the global unsigned tx is reconstructed for PSBTv0 serialization.

The original branch, rebased to the same base commit, is available at https://github.com/achow101/bitcoin/commits/psbt2-orig/ if that makes it easier to work through the changes. Overall, I don't think the diff is too dramatic.

I've also added the fuzz diff as a commit, with some modifications.

This reorder also makes it possible to break this into a couple smaller PRs, if that's desired.

Concept ACK

Glad to see that this PR got some recent momentum after many years. Will take a deeper look within the next days (primarily motivated by [BIP-375](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0375.mediawiki)).

From https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283#issuecomment-4085820365
> This reorder also makes it possible to break this into a couple smaller PRs, if that's desired.

Can the first 5 commits be extracted out of this PR? These are fixes, test improvements and minor refactorings. Pulling them out would get them merged quicker and also make this PR somewhat smaller (from a number of commits POV even if the diff doesn't reduce much).

- [psbt: Fill hash preimages and taproot builder from SignatureData](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283/changes/0dbe6b7b1d8890f1f446f7508d33f1e958045baf)
- [test: construct psbt with unknown field programmatically](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283/changes/4556054508fc1f6fe13e8e1959941873ee6158a1)
- [test: PSBTs should roundtrip through RPCs that do nothing](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283/changes/749760ebfa86eef1e095e13b939072280f4f2c69)
- [psbt: Refactor duplicate key lookup and size checks](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283/changes/6281e58d01284e9af928dfec5eed5316111adf2f)
- [gui: Store PSBT in std::optional in PSBTOperationsDialog](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283/changes/f374fc6f0aa8b83ff48723ef61549a1597444bed)

The actual implementation doesn't seem to start until the 6th commit [psbt: make PSBT structs into classes](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283/changes/e4a1e33fd9622b59f29866077b2a872f7f1959b4).

I verified that the [Ledger bitcoin-app test suite](https://github.com/LedgerHQ/app-bitcoin-new/tree/develop/tests) works against this branch (core compiled at d7834ab5c58c15ff0fe2f0be7ef4986f0e8634d4).

The test suite creates transactions various combinations of with wallets using multisig/miniscript/musig, where a simulated Ledger device is a cosigner, and bitcoin-core is one or several cosigners. Transactions are created with `walletcreatefundedpsbt`, signed with `walletprocesspsbt` (for the core-controlled cosigners), then psbts go through `combinepsbt` and `finalizepsbt`, and resulting rawtx are broadcasted successfully on regtest.

(Note: since version 2.0.0 (late 2021), the client of the Ledger bitcoin app always converted PSBTs to v2 before signing; however, the test suite used whichever psbt version `walletcreatefundedpsbt` returns for the interactions with bitcoin-core - so it's still very nice to see no changes are required for our e2e tests).

> This change makes combinepsbt preserve proprietary fields instead of dropping them during PSBT merge, similar to the #34893

Since that's an orthogonal bug fix, I'll leave it for #34893

Left some more findings below (none of them related to the fuzz crash above though).

events.message:
psbt: Fill hash preimages and taproot builder from SignatureData

Filling these fields was missing.
test: construct psbt with unknown field programmatically
test: PSBTs should roundtrip through RPCs that do nothing

psbt: Refactor duplicate key lookup and size checks

Every key has a duplicate key lookup check, and many keys have fixed
size checks. These can be refactored to reduce code duplication.

Co-Authored-By: David Gumberg <davidzgumberg@gmail.com>

gui: Store PSBT in std::optional in PSBTOperationsDialog

Use std::optional to store the PSBT to avoid having a default constructed PSBT
psbt: make PSBT structs into classes
Have PSBTInput and PSBTOutput know the PSBT's version

psbt: add tx input and output fields in PSBTInput and PSBTOutput

PSBTInput should be aware of the previous txid, output index, and
sequence numbers for inputs, extracting them from the global
unsigned tx.

PSBTOutput should be aware of the output amount and script, extracting
them from the global unsigned tx.

This prepares for PSBTv2 where these fields are serialized.

psbt: Remove default constructor

Instead of allowing PSBTs to be default constructor, force usage of the
deserialization constructor.

CombinePSBTs, DecodeBase64PSBT, and DecodeRawPSBT are all changed to
return std::optional or util::result rather than using an output
parameter to avoid the need for a default constructor.

psbt: add PSBTv2 global tx fields

When decomposing a transaction into a PSBTv2, the tx version and
locktime need to be stored in their respective global fields. Add those
fields and fill them when constructing.

Add PSBTInput::GetOutPoint

Helper for getting the PSBTInput COutPoint
psbt: AddInput and AddOutput should take only PSBTInput and PSBTOutput

psbt: Use PSBTInput and PSBTOutput fields instead of accessing global tx

PSBTInput now has the previous txid and output index, and PSBTOutput has
the amount and script. We no longer need to access the global unsigned
tx for these fields.

Additionally, we can change iterating tx.vin and tx.vout to psbtx.inputs
and psbtx.outputs.

This is in prepration for use with PSBTv2 where the global unsigned tx
will not exist.

Replace PSBT::GetInputUTXO with PSBTInput::GetUTXO

Now that PSBTInput's track their own prevouts, there's no need for a
PSBT global function to fetch input specific data.
psbt: Return std::optional from PrecomputePSBTData

Replace PSBT.tx with PSBT::GetUnsignedTx and PSBT::GetUniqueID

The global unsigned tx is decomposed into separate fields inside of
PSBT, which mirrors what PSBTv2 will do. However, we still need to get
the global unsigned tx so PSBT::GetUnsignedTx is introduced to do that.
In order to also have a stable unique ID, we also introduce
PSBT::GetUniqueID to replace uses of PSBT.tx.GetHash().
Restrict joinpsbts to PSBTv0 only
Implement PSBTv2 fields de/ser
Update test_framework/psbt.py for PSBTv2

Add PSBT::ComputeLockTime()

Function to compute the lock time for the transaction
Implement PSBTv2 in decodepsbt

Allow specifying PSBT version in constructor

The constructor will create the PSBT of the specified version, as long
as it is 0 or 2.
Implement PSBTv2 AddInput and AddOutput
Implement PSBTv2 field merging

events.body: Fixed the fuzz crashes

events.message:
Add psbt_version to PSBT RPCs and default to v2

walletcreatefundedpsbt, createpsbt, converttopsbt, and psbtbumpfee take
a psbt_version argument to set the version of the PSBT that they
produce. The default psbt_version is 2.
psbt: Change default psbt version to 2
tests: Add test vectors from BIP 370
tests: Add PSBT unit test for ComputeTimeLock
test: Tests for PSBT AddInput and AddOutput
fuzz: Enforce additional version invariants in PSBT fuzzer
doc: Release notes for psbtv2

comments.body: 09c8656aaa27d18e2d14a52cdd67ee46f6a5cae6: note to other reviewers: you can run both through `decodepsbt` and notice the "unknown" key `f0010203040506070809` in the first vector, `f0010203040506070810` in the second and both appear in the combined "result" below.

6a73e374fb0835dd06433e079c7c9300d6412a11 nit: `variable 'found_input_count' set but not used [-Werror,-Wunused-but-set-variable]`

Same for `found_output_count`.

The "test-each-commit" job doesn't catch this, because it's limited to the last 6 commits.

Although `PSBT_GLOBAL_INPUT_COUNT` case does set it, it's not enough for the compiler to be happy. It wants you to introduce these variables in c6743bf7137615eb20145c6eb3e2da5c82903b1e. Doing so also increases clarity imo.

---

Fwiw all later commits compile fine.

6a73e374fb0835dd06433e079c7c9300d6412a11: as I noted in https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/31247#discussion_r1924976381 it would be really nice if we can abstract this duplicate and error prone size checking stuff into e.g. a DSL that defines it for all fields.

6a73e374fb0835dd06433e079c7c9300d6412a11: IIUC `UnserializeFromVector` checks that the first byte of `s` represents a size and that it matches the number of bytes left in in `s`.

So that protects against passing in _too many_ bytes. But does it protect against too few bytes?

The `UnserializeMany` method uses `std::make_shared` which calls the constructor for `Txid` (in this case), which in return, I think, picks the `uint256(Span<const unsigned char> vch)` constructor. Which doesn't check the length.

Maybe `UnserializeMany` could do a sizeof on its `args`?
This PR is big enough already, I'd prefer to do that separately.

No... that's not at all how deserialization works. There's no constructors called, `prev_txid` is an already initialized `uint256`. Unserializing into it means that the data is overwritten. There's no `std::make_shared` called anywhere, nothing is constructed or initialized.

The call chain is `UnserializeFromVector` -> `UnserializeMany` -> `uint256::Unserialize()` -> `Unserialize(Stream& s, Span<B> span)` -> `<template Stream> read()`, and every `read(Span)` function checks that the stream has at least `span.size()` bytes, and reads exactly `span.size()` bytes into the span.

Unserializing uint256 always reads exactly 32 bytes, that's why no hashes ever need to be length prefixed. The same applies here.
Agreed.
Thanks for clarifying. So in that case both too many and too few bytes are prevented.

In 2dc96bd4dc5169f692b94ae98eeb6bcd6b0f1ee9 "Have PSBTInput and PSBTOutput know the PSBT's version", nit: calling this param `psbt_version` makes it clear that inputs and outputs don't have their own versioning. And consistent with the eventual variable name.
In https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/commit/2dc96bd4dc5169f692b94ae98eeb6bcd6b0f1ee9 "Have PSBTInput and PSBTOutput know the PSBT's version", μnit `const`.

In 2dc96bd4dc5169f692b94ae98eeb6bcd6b0f1ee9 "Have PSBTInput and PSBTOutput know the PSBT's version", nit: `/*psbt_version=*/` would be useful here, but this goes away in a later commit (and before the default is changed to 2).

In 2dc96bd4dc5169f692b94ae98eeb6bcd6b0f1ee9 "Have PSBTInput and PSBTOutput know the PSBT's version": somewhere later in the PR, we should add a target for version 2?

Or better: any version, which would detect that the `PSBTInput` constructor doesn't prevent version 1 (not currently a problem, but could creep in if we build functionality to add inputs to a PSBT).
In c6743bf7137615eb20145c6eb3e2da5c82903b1e "Enforce PSBT version constraints": this is hard to read. Would prefer two separate checks based on the PSBT version.
In cef9de00f4c2d4c9c033e7dce2b7ffac2e694a26 "Call CacheUnsignedTxPieces in PSBT constructor": might as well do this in the previous commit. Ditto for the next commit.

In 0c459e43ba1ce74d2e88dde352e28a63743f4746 "Add PSBT::CacheUnsignedTxPieces": this seems fine, but it would be good to have a method that verifies that for v0 PSBTs these two representations are in sync. You could then enable that method in debug builds and call it in a bunch of places.

The could e.g call `GetUnsignedTx()` (introduced later), skip its `if (tx != std::nullopt)` early return, and compare.

In 162d0916774c719f1e3749b0d4d204e296ee4ac8 "Add PSBT::GetUniqueID": I think this should check the version number, and then `Assume(version == 0 || tx == std::nullopt)`

The way it's written now suggests `tx` is just a cache that might be stale.
In aeb9e44bc5a221553d2010752c76f8f16205551c "Change PSBT::AddInput to take just PSBTInput": I think it's more readable to just check the PSBT version directly.
In https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/commit/aeb9e44bc5a221553d2010752c76f8f16205551c "Change PSBT::AddInput to take just PSBTInput": `assert(false)` ?
In https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/commit/aeb9e44bc5a221553d2010752c76f8f16205551c "Change PSBT::AddInput to take just PSBTInput": could add `// Prevent duplicate input`

In https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/commit/aeb9e44bc5a221553d2010752c76f8f16205551c "Change PSBT::AddInput to take just PSBTInput":

```
// Avoid duplicate input
[psbtin](const PSBTInput& psbt_input) {
```

(the comment about duplicate inputs is introduced in 3fe17931ae2d0e1b83cbe9c376f5d255ac9de68d "Implement PSBTv2 AddInput and AddOutput")

But this only works for v2, since `prev_txid` is nullptr otherwise. That's why the duplicate input check for v0 is maintained below.

It's more clear if you explicitly check the PSBT version in both places.
In a79e519323ee35544e854efd6cbf7ce8577fd4e3 "Implement PSBTv2 field merging": `Assume(...) || return false;`?

comments.body: Should this be ‎`psbt.o` instead of ‎`psbt.i` here?
PSBT_OUT_SCRIPT is not required in [BIP375](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0375.mediawiki#specification). Should we address that here, or is it out of scope for this PR?

This is incorrect because for version 0, `m_version` may also be `nullopt`. This explains the different unique IDs i reported earlier.

It seems you had it right in other conditionals, such as in `AddInput()` where you have:
```
if (m_version == std::nullopt || m_version == 0) {
```

In other places you simply check whether the `tx` member is `nullopt` as a proxy of checking for V2.

So i would recommend having a method that is simply `IsV0()` (or alternatively `IsV2()`) and use it everywhere.
No, that is out of scope for this PR.

We would happily parse an all-0 txid, but not serialize it? I think we should be consistent. Using an optional as for other fields would allow to distinguish absence of prev_txid key and all-0 prev_txid.
copypasta typo: s/time/height/
nit: should those be `explicit`?
Here and below are other instances of the incorrect version check https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283#discussion_r2879797382
nit: meh at caching the presence in a boolean to do the check after parsing, rather than doing the checks right at parsing times like for pre-existing fields.

This makes sense to require that fields are mutually exclusive, but this is not specified by the BIP. In fact it seems to hint that it is explicitly supported:
> If a PSBT has both types of locktimes possible because one or more inputs specify both PSBT_IN_REQUIRED_TIME_LOCKTIME and PSBT_IN_REQUIRED_HEIGHT_LOCKTIME, then locktime determined by looking at the PSBT_IN_REQUIRED_HEIGHT_LOCKTIME fields of the inputs must be chosen
Hmm this assert can be triggered since we will happily parse a PSBT with an input containing both a time-based locktime and a height-based locktime?
nit: move it?

UB'ing if the passed `PSBTInput` has no prevout, which even its default constructor doesn't set, is quite the footgun. Especially as this precondition comes entirely undocumented..

Since AddInput already returns bool, could you just return false when it's not set?
Can you return false instead of UB'ing when expected fields aren't set?

Hmm are we sure we want the union of sets of modifiable bits, rather than the intersection? For instance if you merge a PSBT with an ACP signature and only its inputs-modifiable bit set, with a PSBT with a NONE signature and only its outputs-modifiable bit set, then i think you want as a result a PSBT with none of those bits set.

This is annoying because you want the opposite semantics for the SIGHASH_SINGLE bit it seems: if any of the PSBT has a SIGHASH_SINGLE sig, the result should set the has-single bit.

Should `Merge` on inputs and outputs return a boolean like the method on the top-level PSBT? Crashing with no documented pre-condition is not great, and triggering UB on a missing expected field is worse.

nit: Those call sites could have been updated in the same commit they were introduced. In this PR you often have one commit introducing a method, and then one or more commits updating various call sites. I think it's unnecessary but not a big deal, but i'm curious why you took this approach?
This mutant should exist in master currently, and I think fixing it is out of scope for this PR.
This mutant should exist in master currently, and I think fixing it is out of scope for this PR.

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+Updated RPCs
+------------
+
+- `createpsbt` and `walletcreatepsbt` will now default to creating
+  version 2 PSBTs. An optional `psbt_version` argument is added to
+  both which allows specifying the version of PSBT to create.
comments.body: Also mention the RPCs that will now always return v2 PSBTs? Some downstream project may be relying on being able to parse the output as a v0 PSBT.
That the tx member is present when PSBT version is 0 is an invariant that you already rely on throughout this PR. What is different in this instance?

This caching is necessary in order to check against the version number because the field containing the version number may be serialized after the input and output counts. In general, for the constraints on other fields within a scope must be checked after everything has be deserialized as fields may be serialized in any order within a scope.

The fields are not mutually exclusive. This line is not enforcing mutual exclusion, `height_lock` is used for computing the tx's locktime. What's happening here is that if an input says it can only take a time based locktime, then the tx cannot have a height based locktime, hence resetting `height_lock` to indicate that that is no longer allowed in later inputs. But an input could still specify both this condition would not be triggered in that case.
I'll leave it for a followup. That still expands the scope of the PR, and makes the diff bigger.
This is changed to check against the version
Fixed
Done
Done
Fixed

Yes.

I've ended up changing `GetUnsignedTx()` to return `std::optional<CMutableTransaction>` to deal with failure in this case.
No longer relevant
This is fixed as all `PSBTInput` must have `prev_txid` and `prev_out`
No longer relevant

Right.

I've changed this to AND the flags in general, except for the SIGHASH_SINGLE bit.
Done

Commits have been reordered and squashed to do this.

The original order was because introducing many of the helper functions was required before it was possible to update all of the call sites. However, the new order moves the PSBTv2 fields serialization later in the PR which makes it easier to update these call sites beforehand when other fields are introduced.

@@ -107,37 +111,76 @@ def __init__(self, *, g=None, i=None, o=None):

         self.i = i if i is not None else []

     def deserialize(self, f):

         self.g = from_binary(PSBTMap, f)

comments.body: in bde5ca901cba798713892c479b645bc0fea8f4a4: I think these two checks should be moved to the `if self.version == 2` branch below, as `PSBT_GLOBAL_VERSION` might be present and explicitly be set to zero

in 6281e58d01284e9af928dfec5eed5316111adf2f: strictly speaking that's not a refactor; instead of a type name the user now only sees a hex-string for duplicate keys (OTOH it includes the key data, so it's in some sense more verbose, but less human-readable than before); non-blocking I guess, if really considered relevant the error message could be improved in a follow-up (with e.g. a key type -> string lookup table)

in a2874df534b649a13f427f84fccd371f0e2a48fc: IIUC from https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21283#discussion_r2879797382, the motivation for doing the version check with the "less than" operator was to catch both `std::nullopt` and 0. Now that `GetVersion` doesn't return an `std::optional` anymore, it could be changed back to `GetVersion() == 0`. Currently it looks very strange IMHO and implies that 1 would also be a valid version

in 73018392b7d7c2f1da4dfbd438b6d0ff3bf5dfb8: this looks like a bug and likely was meant to be
```suggestion
        m_tx_modifiable = final_modifiable;
```
(probably worth it to extend the BIP370 test vectors with cases for merging that would have caught this)
I'll leave that for a followup

The reason for using "<" is to implicitly use the v0 behavior if a v1 PSBT were to ever make it to this code. I didn't want to put special handling for v1 everywhere that version numbers were checked. Although it was never specified, my expectation is that if a v1 PSBT ever existed, it would be treated the same way as a v0 PSBT.

-        self.o = [from_binary(PSBTMap, f) for _ in self.tx.vout]

+        self.version = 0
+        if PSBT_GLOBAL_VERSION in self.g.map:
+            assert PSBT_GLOBAL_INPUT_COUNT in self.g.map
+            assert PSBT_GLOBAL_OUTPUT_COUNT in self.g.map

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+Updated RPCs
+------------
+
+- `createpsbt` and `walletcreatepsbt` will now default to creating
+  version 2 PSBTs. An optional `psbt_version` argument is added to
+  both which allows specifying the version of PSBT to create.
comments.body: The release notes don't mention `converttopsbt`, which now always creates v2 PSBTs (not sure if a `psbt_version` argument is needed for this one as well? but in either case a mentioning in the release notes would make sense).

As the comment suggests, not calculating the locktime is intentional.

But I forgot that I wrote that the locktime needs to be calculated for unique ID: https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0370.mediawiki#unique-identification
Fixed
